summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #554 where users with local LLM servers (llama.cpp) see a generic 'Service temporarily unavailable' error instead of the real error when validating providers in Settings > LLMs.
summary.outcome: All three planned changes were implemented and verified: error code mapping fixed in backend, server error message surfaced in frontend validation, and model discovery timeout increased. All relevant tests passed and changes were committed.

repo: ServiceError::Forbidden maps to FORBIDDEN; all other ServiceErrors previously mapped to UNAVAILABLE (semantically wrong — UNAVAILABLE is for node connectivity, INTERNAL is for request-processing errors).

validateProviderKey() used res?.error?.message (localized) while testModel() already used res?.error?.serverMessage (raw). The serverMessage field is the correct one for surfacing real errors to users.
The justfile lint recipe excludes moltis-providers and moltis-gateway from --all-features and runs them separately with --features local-llm-metal to avoid CUDA issues on macOS.
fetch_models_from_api() in openai.rs had an 8s timeout while the probe timeout was 30s — mismatch caused local LLM servers to fail model discovery during slow loads.

code.finding: From<ServiceError> for ErrorShape impl — changed default arm from UNAVAILABLE to INTERNAL error code.
validateProviderKey() error string — added res?.error?.serverMessage as preferred source before falling back to localized res?.error?.message.
fetch_models_from_api() timeout changed from Duration::from_secs(8) to Duration::from_secs(15).

workflow: Running cargo tests for multiple crates in parallel as background tasks significantly speeds up verification.
When just lint fails on macOS due to CUDA environment issues, running clippy directly on only the modified crates is a valid substitute to confirm changes are lint-clean.
Biome is used for JS linting/formatting in this project (not ESLint/Prettier).

friction: just lint failed with a pre-existing CUDA build failure on macOS (llama-cpp-sys-2 with CUDA features pulled in via --all-features on some workspace crate), requiring a manual workaround to verify only the modified crates.
Background tests required polling task output files to check results rather than getting them inline.
open_items: The pre-existing just lint CUDA failure on macOS is unresolved — some crate in the workspace outside the exclusion list is still pulling in CUDA features via --all-features.
Manual QA verification (configuring an unreachable provider endpoint and checking the error displayed) was not performed — left as a manual step.
